data.title: U.S. White House Spokesperson: Allies Support Trump in Opening Hormuz Strait
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has received backing from allies regarding the strategic decision to open the Hormuz Strait, according to Jin10. The White House spokesperson emphasized the importance of maintaining open navigation in this critical maritime passage, which is vital for global oil transportation. The support from allied nations underscores the collaborative efforts to ensure stability and security in the region. The Hormuz Strait, a key chokepoint for oil shipments, has been a focal point of geopolitical tensions, and the U.S. administration's move aims to safeguard international trade routes.

data.title: EU Chooses Lille, France as Location for EU Customs Authority, Cyprus Announces
data.description: The European Union has selected Lille, France, as the location for its new EU Customs Authority, according to an announcement by Cyprus, the current holder of the EU's rotating presidency. According to Jin10, this decision marks a significant step in the EU's efforts to streamline and enhance its customs operations across member states. The establishment of the EU Customs Authority in Lille is expected to facilitate better coordination and efficiency in customs procedures, benefiting trade and economic activities within the EU. The choice of Lille underscores the EU's commitment to strengthening its customs infrastructure and improving regulatory oversight. This move is part of broader efforts to ensure seamless trade operations and enhance the EU's competitiveness in the global market.

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Appoint Business Leaders to Technology Advisory Panel
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump plans to appoint several prominent business leaders to a technology advisory committee. According to Odaily, the committee will include Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg, Oracle founder Larry Ellison, and NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang. These members are expected to provide insights on artificial intelligence policies and other related topics.

White House officials have disclosed that the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST) will be co-chaired by White House AI and cryptocurrency advisor Andrew Sacks and technology advisor Michael Kratsios. Officials stated that Trump intends to appoint the first 13 industry members on Wednesday, including Google co-founder Sergey Brin. The committee, as outlined in an executive order, may eventually comprise up to 24 members.

data.title: Non-USD Stablecoin Supply Reaches $1.1 Billion in February, Euro Stablecoins Dominate Market Cap
data.description: A recent report by Visa and Dune highlights significant growth in the non-USD stablecoin market. According to NS3.AI, the supply of these stablecoins reached $1.1 billion in February, with transfer volumes soaring to $10 billion. The report reveals that over 1.2 million addresses are holding these tokens, with substantial balances found in user wallets, centralized exchanges, and institutional treasuries. Euro stablecoins constitute more than 80% of the market cap, while Circle's EURC is responsible for over 90% of the non-USD stablecoin transfer volume.

data.title: South African Business Community Protests Following Lawyer's Assassination
data.description: The recent assassination of a labor lawyer associated with Sibanye Stillwater in downtown Johannesburg has sparked a protest from South Africa's business community. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing concerns over safety and security in the region. The incident has intensified calls for improved measures to protect professionals and ensure a safer working environment. The business fraternity is urging authorities to take swift action to address the rising violence and safeguard the interests of those working in the sector.

data.title: Germany's Finance Minister Announces Income Tax Reform to Benefit 95% of Workers
data.description: Germany's Finance Minister has announced plans for an income tax reform aimed at benefiting 95% of workers. According to Jin10, the reform is part of a broader strategy to encourage increased productivity among the German workforce. The minister emphasized the need for Germans to work harder to sustain economic growth and competitiveness. This initiative is expected to provide financial relief to a significant portion of the population, potentially boosting consumer spending and economic activity. The details of the reform are yet to be fully disclosed, but it marks a significant step in Germany's fiscal policy adjustments.

data.title: Oracle Receives Buy Rating from Bank of America Analyst with 30% Upside Potential
data.description: Bank of America analyst Tal Liani has reinstated Oracle with a Buy rating and set a price target of $200 as of March 24. According to NS3.AI, this suggests a potential upside of approximately 30% from current levels. The analyst's optimistic outlook is linked to Oracle's impressive 84% year-over-year growth in cloud revenue during the last quarter. However, Liani also highlighted concerns regarding heavy capital expenditure and execution risks that could impact Oracle's future performance.

data.title: Coinbase will launch spot trading of Perle (PRL)
data.description: Coinbase announced that spot trading of Perle (PRL) will launch on March 25, 2026. If liquidity conditions are met and trading is supported, the PRL-USD trading pair will open later today.

data.title: Auto Retailer Tricolor's Collapse Leaves Buyers in Financial Uncertainty
data.description: The collapse of auto retailer Tricolor last fall has left thousands of buyers, many of whom are immigrants from Latin America, in a state of financial uncertainty. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges these buyers face in managing their expensive loans. The sudden shutdown of Tricolor has raised concerns about the financial stability of its customers, who are now grappling with the burden of repaying their loans without the support of the retailer. This situation underscores the broader issues within the auto retail industry, particularly for vulnerable communities relying on such services for vehicle purchases. The impact of Tricolor's bankruptcy continues to unfold, leaving affected buyers seeking solutions to their financial predicaments.

data.title: Foreign Powers Assess Implications of Ongoing Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict has prompted foreign powers to evaluate its implications on global dynamics. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting discussions led by experts such as Tim O'Brien, Marc Champion, and Liam Denning. These discussions focus on the potential conclusions and strategic adjustments that other nations might consider in response to the unfolding situation. The analysis aims to provide insights into how international relations and geopolitical strategies could be reshaped by the current events.

data.title: China's Influence on Iran Regarding Strait of Hormuz Questioned
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X, raising questions about China's potential pressure on Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz. The discussion involves Tim O'Brien, Marc Champion, and Liam Denning, who are examining the geopolitical implications of such actions. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical maritime passage for global oil shipments, and any changes in its accessibility could have significant impacts on international trade and energy markets. The conversation highlights the strategic interests of China in the region and the broader implications for global economic stability.

data.title: Movement launches its native stablecoin USDCx on its mainnet.
data.description: Movement Network has officially launched its native stablecoin USDCx on its mainnet. Backed by the CirclexReserve protocol, this stablecoin is pegged 1:1 to USDC, boasts near-zero transaction fees, and requires no third-party cross-chain bridges. Users automatically receive USDCx after crossing their USDC to Movement. All exchanges and transfers are verified by the xReserve contract, and the reserve assets are transparently verifiable on-chain. USDCx also interoperates with over 30 public chains using Circle CCTP and Gateway, providing cross-ecosystem liquidity. USDCx is positioned for large-scale user applications, including DeFi liquidity (providing a native stablecoin for DEXs and lending protocols), cross-border payments and remittances (reducing transfer costs in emerging markets), and the development of new digital banking and fintech infrastructure.

data.title: Bitmine has officially launched MAVAN, an institutional-grade Ethereum staking platform, with over 3.14 million ETH currently staked.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Ethereum treasury company Bitmine announced the official launch of its institutional-grade Ethereum staking platform, MAVAN (Made in America Validator Network), designed to provide institutions with a highly secure, high-performance, and resilient staking infrastructure. MAVAN combines US-based validator nodes with a globally distributed architecture, meeting the needs of institutions requiring compliant domestic infrastructure while also supporting global client access. Initially designed to support Bitmine's own Ethereum asset allocation, the platform will be opened to institutional investors, custodians, and ecosystem partners in the future. As of March 24, 2026, Bitmine had staked approximately 3.1426 million ETH (approximately $6.8 billion). (PRNewswire)

data.title: NY Civil Case Raises Concerns Over Business Asset Freeze
data.description: Blockchain sleuth ZachXBT posted on X, expressing concerns over the sealed New York civil case involving Circle and Fast Company. He highlighted the lack of transparency, noting that authorities have provided no justification for freezing the assets of these businesses. The absence of a clear basis for such actions has raised questions about the implications for the companies involved and the broader business community. ZachXBT's comments underscore the need for clarity and accountability in legal proceedings that impact business operations.

data.title: Oil Market Faces Challenges Due to Hormuz Strait Tensions
data.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains a critical point of tension for the global oil market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting expert opinions on the strategic importance of this narrow passage. Liam Denning, an energy analyst, described the situation as a 'cosmic joke' affecting oil dynamics. The strait, a vital conduit for oil shipments, has been a focal point of geopolitical tensions, impacting market stability. Analysts emphasize the need for vigilance as any disruption could have significant repercussions on global oil supply and prices.

data.title: Swiss Government to Ease Bank Capital Rules for UBS
data.description: The Swiss government is set to introduce new bank capital regulations next month, which are expected to be less stringent for UBS compared to existing proposals, according to analysts from Bank of America. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the anticipated changes that could impact the financial landscape for UBS. The adjustments come amid ongoing discussions about the balance between regulatory oversight and financial stability in the banking sector. Analysts suggest that the revised rules may offer UBS more flexibility in managing its capital requirements, potentially influencing its strategic operations and market positioning. The Swiss government's decision reflects a broader trend of reassessing financial regulations to ensure they align with current economic conditions and the needs of major financial institutions.

data.title: Pakistan's Role in U.S.-Iran Diplomacy Gains Attention
data.description: Pakistan has emerged as a significant player in the ongoing diplomatic efforts between the United States and Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's newfound importance in the geopolitical landscape. Analysts are closely monitoring the developments, as Pakistan's involvement could influence the dynamics of U.S.-Iran relations. The situation remains fluid, with experts providing live analysis to understand the implications of Pakistan's role in the diplomacy. Observers are keen to see how this will affect regional stability and international diplomatic strategies.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | International Spot Gold Shows Dual Peaks in Chip Accumulation
data.description: International spot gold is exhibiting a distinct pattern of chip accumulation, divided into two segments: a low peak before the breakout and a high peak after the breakout. According to Jin10, the lower edge of the high peak, marked at 45XX, has emerged as a critical decision price. There is a possibility that the price may move downward to fill the gap.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $40.8003 million yesterday, marking the fifth consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $40.8003 million yesterday (March 24th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $2.1813 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $163 million. This was followed by the 21Shares ETF (TETH), with a net inflow of $1.0594 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $15.5675 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net outflow of $24.9741 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.871 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $12.221 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.71%, and a cumulative net inflow of $11.672 billion.

data.title: Hungary's Household Sentiment Sees Modest Improvement in March
data.description: Hungary's household sentiment experienced a slight uplift in March, coinciding with Prime Minister Viktor Orban's pre-election spending measures. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that the government's financial initiatives aimed at boosting consumer confidence ahead of the elections have had a limited impact. Despite these efforts, the overall mood among Hungarian households remains cautious, reflecting ongoing economic challenges. The government's spending strategy is part of a broader attempt to stabilize the economy and garner support in the upcoming electoral period. However, the modest improvement suggests that more comprehensive measures may be necessary to achieve significant economic recovery.
